temporal_description:
A close-up shot focuses on the face of a man in a dimly lit, blue-toned room. He has dark hair and appears distressed, with a pained expression. He is looking down and to his right. On his left wrist, a watch is visible. White Chinese characters appear at the bottom of the screen, reading "不知道" (Don't know). In the top right corner, a watermark with the text "风行网" is present. The audio opens with a man speaking in a low, dejected, and slightly shaky voice, saying, "不知道" (Don't know).

The camera pulls back to a wider shot, revealing a tense meeting in a dark, wood-paneled room. Three men are seated around a large table. On the right, a man in a light-colored suit and glasses sits behind the table, looking down at a briefcase. In the center, with his back to the camera, a man in a white shirt sits with his arms crossed. To the left, another man in a blue shirt is also seated. A fourth man, wearing a plaid shirt, enters the room from a doorway in the background and walks toward the table. The "风行网" watermark remains in the top right corner. Another man's voice, firm and authoritative, cuts in, "长官，查到了" (Sir, we've found it). His tone is direct and reports a successful investigation. The first man then speaks again, his voice still low and filled with a sense of weary resignation, as if explaining a difficult discovery: "原来那两个洋人是私家侦探" (Turns out those two foreigners were private detectives). The background is quiet, with no music, emphasizing the serious and tense atmosphere of the conversation.
